sections.paragraph:
θέμα, ατος, τό (s. τίθημι; Cebes; Plut.; ins, pap, LXX; SibOr 2, 46; 49) orig. ‘deposit of money’; in our lit. specif. a prize offered contestants, in so far as it consists of money, not a wreath (SIG 1063, 21 w. note 4; OGI 339, 81f; 566, 28) θ. ἀφθαρσία the prize is immortality IPol 2:3 (Zahn in his edition unnecessarily changed ἀφθαρσία, which is the rdg. of the mss., to the gen.). θέμα λίαν καλόν exceptionally fine prize 1 Tim 6:19 cj.—DELG s.v. τίθημι.

Cebes Cebes , prominent in Platonic dialogues; ref. is to a dialogue associated with his name, I a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

orig. orig. = original(ly)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

specif. specif. = specifically

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. cj. = conjecture(d by)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 448
